We have to propose a fix plan. Must use only provided tools: run_cmd, read_file, write_file. Follow the workflow: first output a plan, then call review_plan. The plan must be full description. Then we wait for review_plan result. The user wants us to remediate an OpenSCAP finding: auditd Collects System Administrator Actions - /etc/sudoers.d/. They want to add audit rule for /etc/sudoers.d/ -w /etc/sudoers.d/ -p wa -k actions. We must check whether augenrules is used (use_augenrules=1). So we need to read /etc/audit/auditd.conf to see use_augenrules. If use_augenrules=1, we should create /etc/audit/rules.d/sudoers.rules with that line. Ensure ownership and permissions root:root 0600. If use_augenrules=0, we should modify /etc/audit/audit.rules. Also need ensure no duplicates. Need to restart/reload auditd. And maybe verify with auditctl -l.

We also must not chain commands. Must use single commands. Use read_file to inspect target config file before modifying it. Use sed -i to modify existing values. Don't duplicate config lines.

For consistency maybe use sed to replace line if exists commented or not. If use_augenrules=0: modify /etc/audit/audit.rules similarly. Use sed to add line or uncomment.

Also after making changes, send systemctl reload auditd. Then run auditctl -l | grep line. We don't chain commands, so we need separate run_cmd calls.
